titleStmt.title: No. 895Memorandum by the United States Chiefs of Staff

div.head:
No. 895
Memorandum by the United States Chiefs of Staff Considered by the Combined Chiefs of Staff at their 197th Meeting, July 20. See ante, p. 162.

p.hi: Disposal of Enemy War Materiel in Germany and Austria

div.p: CCS 706/11 document No. 361, printed in vol. i .
The United States Chiefs of Staff recommend that the Combined Chiefs of Staff approve Appendices “A” and “B” to C. C. S. 706/11 as amended herein.

frus:attachment.p: Words underscored are proposed additions. Words italicized are proposed deletions. [Footnote in the source copy.] Message to Supreme Commander, Allied Expeditionary Force,General of the Army Dwight D. Eisenhower. and to the Supreme Allied Commander, Mediterranean, Field Marshal Sir Harold Alexander. from the Combined Chiefs of Staff; to the Commanding General, United States Forces, European Theater,General of the Army Dwight D. Eisenhower. from the United States Chiefs of Staff; and to the Commander in Chief, British Forces of Occupation in Germany, Field Marshal Sir Bernard Montgomery. from the British Chiefs of Staff.

The London Munitions Assignments Board is being authorized to assign surrendered or captured enemy materiel (unless it is of non-German origin) in the hands of Britishor American-commanded forces in Austria and Germany to the forces of those countries employed in furtherance of the agreed strategy in the war against Japan. After the dissolution of Supreme Headquarters, Allied Expeditionary Force, unless agreement has been reached in the Control Council, the respective Chiefs of Staff will issue further instructions to the commanding officers of the British and United States zones of occupation with respect to treatment of enemy materiel which is in excess of the requirements of the military commanders and in excess of the materiel needed to fill the requirements of the London Munitions Assignments Board. Pending the receipt of such instructions, no such materiel should be destroyed except in so far as such destruction is required for military and public security.
The above message is in reply to Scaf 447,See vol. i, document No. 361, appendix D. which is being repeated to the Supreme Allied Commander, Mediterranean, today.

p.hi: Message to the London Munitions Assignments Board

frus:attachment.p:
Until the countries which are members of the European Advisory Commission have agreed upon a disposal policy, and until such an agreed policy is implemented, the London Munitions Assignments Board is authorized to assign surrendered or captured enemy materiel (unless it is of non-German origin)Words underscored are proposed additions. [Footnote in the source copy.] in the hands of British- or
American-commanded forces in Austria and Germany to the forces of those countries which will use such materiel in furtherance of the agreed strategy in the war against Japan.
